title: 有翼之暗
summary:
Woodside asks Inaba for advice on where the killer might have hidden a head. Inaba suggests the location is "Hell's Gate," a room in the mansion. Jiban, the son of a neighbor, leads the group—including Kaname, Mutsu, the narrator, Detective Horikawa, Kumagai, Kumozawa, Wakamatsu, Wakuzaki, Tsubaki, Detective Toriyama, Sugayan, and Kanou—through a dark, narrow, and unsettling passage toward the room.

The group arrives at a thick, ivory-colored marble door featuring exquisite carvings of the torments of hell, including naked bodies and an inverted Eastern Orthodox cross. Kumozawa observes the artistry, while Wakamatsu suggests the door was not originally intended for use as a door. Wakuzaki uses an oil lamp to inspect the upper part of the door, discovering small, soybean-sized holes recessed into the carvings. Detective Toriyama attempts to open the door, but finds it locked. Sugayan produces a key and successfully unlocks the door.

The narrative then shifts to a prologue set the previous day. Yuki Hikari, the narrator, visits an office where Mukai and Kumazawa are present. Kumazawa has opened two envelopes addressed to Murakami Yusuke: one white and one light brown. The brown envelope is covered in a white powder used for fingerprint extraction. The letters have no sender. It is noted that Murakami's father runs the Murakami Detective Agency, an elite and reputable firm in the Kyoto and Kansai regions.
